description: Lock the corrected chronology and causal boundaries for the Renaissance source-fit batch.

checks.reason: The National Archives dates the surviving Royal Navy officers' log series from around 1669.
The archival evidence documents navigational records rather than a papermaking innovation.
Position, course, and movement entries directly use navigational practice.
Museum Plantin-Moretus and the Library of Congress date Ortelius's first atlas to 1570.
The 1570 atlas cannot depend on the separately scoped 1588 machine-book convention.
KU Leuven documents its first printed university textbook in 1474.
The textbook practice predates and does not require scientific illustration.
Philosophical Transactions volume 1 includes a standalone errata notice in 1665.
The scoped correction notice is journal-native rather than textbook-derived.
The reviewed history dates the documented Venetian prefabricated-parts demonstration to Henry III's 1574 visit.
The Arsenale's docks and sequential sheds formed the production infrastructure.
The later drawing node is not documented in the Venetian production process.
Venetian galley and warship production is independent of Iberian galleon design.
Bourne's table-based printed gunnery manual first appeared in 1578.
The British Museum dates Schlottheim's Mechanical Galleon to circa 1585.
The documented object uses integrated clockwork rather than stage machinery.
